close_reason: Fixed: TmuxSession::create() now launches /bin/bash then delivers command via send-keys (coder/terminal-session @ 7351087). Session ID validated to [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ to prevent tmux addressing issues.
description: HIGH finding from mxdx-1jv security review. TmuxSession::create() passes TerminalSessionRequestEvent.command as tmux new-session final arg. tmux shell-execs this via /bin/sh, enabling shell injection if command contains metacharacters. Fix: launch fixed shell (/bin/bash) in session and deliver user command via send-keys after creation, OR validate command against allowlist+validate_args() before create_session().
title: [FIX] tmux new-session shell-executes command — injection risk

See mxdx-management-console.md §5 and docs/plans/...Tasks 8.1-8.2.

close_reason: Upgraded matrix-sdk 0.8->0.16 and ruma 0.10->0.14 in workspace Cargo.toml. All 3 CVEs addressed.
description:
cargo audit found 3 vulnerabilities in matrix-sdk 0.8.0 (currently pinned in workspace Cargo.toml on feat/scaffold):

VULNERABILITIES (3):
1. RUSTSEC-2025-0135 (matrix-sdk-base 0.8.0): DoS via custom m.room.join_rules events. Fix: upgrade to >=0.16.0
2. RUSTSEC-2025-0065 (matrix-sdk-base 0.8.0): Panic in RoomMember::normalized_power_level(). Fix: upgrade to >=0.14.1
3. RUSTSEC-2025-0041 (matrix-sdk-crypto 0.8.0, CVSS 4.9 MEDIUM): Encrypted event sender spoofing by homeserver admin. Fix: upgrade to >=0.11.1

WARNINGS (2):
4. RUSTSEC-2025-0012: backoff 0.4.0 is unmaintained (transitive dep of matrix-sdk 0.8.0)
5. RUSTSEC-2024-0384: instant 0.1.13 is unmaintained (transitive dep of backoff)

ACTION: Update workspace Cargo.toml:
  matrix-sdk: 0.8 -> 0.16 (latest stable as of 2026-03-05)
  ruma: 0.10 -> 0.14 (latest stable, required for compat with matrix-sdk 0.16)

FEATURE FLAG VERIFICATION (matrix-sdk 0.16):
- 'e2e-encryption': STILL EXISTS, now ENABLED BY DEFAULT. Can be kept explicit or removed from features list.
- 'sqlite': STILL EXISTS, also ENABLED BY DEFAULT. Same — can keep explicit or remove.
- 'qrcode': NEW DEFAULT — enabled by default in 0.16 (used for QR code verification, relevant to key verification flow in mxdx).
- 'bundled-sqlite': NOT default — use this instead of 'sqlite' if system SQLite is unavailable.
- ruma feature flags ('client-api-c', 'events', 'appservice-api-c'): verify against ruma 0.14 Cargo.toml after upgrade.

This is especially critical for mxdx because:
- RUSTSEC-2025-0041 (sender spoofing) directly undermines E2EE trust model that mxdx depends on
- RUSTSEC-2025-0135 (DoS via join_rules) could be used to crash Policy Agent or Launcher
- RUSTSEC-2025-0065 (power level panic) could crash components using RoomMember APIs
title: CRITICAL: matrix-sdk 0.8.0 has 3 known CVEs — upgrade to >=0.16.0

close_reason: Closed
description: HIGH: decode_decompress() has no size limit. A malicious compressed payload can expand to gigabytes causing OOM. Fix in mxdx-cvg (Terminal Subsystem): enforce MAX_DECOMPRESSED_SIZE (default 1MB, configurable) in decode_decompress(). If exceeded, return Err and send TerminalErrorEvent. Test: zlib bomb (10KB → >1MB) is rejected.
title: [FIX] Zlib bomb protection in terminal data decompression before PTY write

description: The two_instances_can_federate test is #[ignore] because tuwunel v1.5 requires TLS for federation (Matrix spec). To enable this test in CI: either (a) set up mkcert to generate trusted local certs and configure tuwunel to use them, or (b) run federation tests in a dedicated job with a docker-compose setup that includes a reverse proxy with TLS. Track as follow-up to Phase 0 test infrastructure.
title: [FIX] Federation tests require TLS — set up mkcert or self-signed certs for local federation CI
